architecture_fit.note: Produces A-AND-NOT-B attention through a real softmax, but reads oracle feat_A/feat_B and injects keys via pinv, never using e_A/e_B — sidesteps the goal's superposition question.

baseline_comparison.note: Includes the framework linear baseline at chance (0.5) and shows a clean 0.5 lift, but the win is achieved via oracle label access so the comparison is hollow.

faithfulness.note: Hand-built synthetic circuit with no ablation; mechanism forces exact keys with pinv from ground-truth labels rather than reading superposed features, and proposes no causal check.

operating_range.note: Flat 1.0 sharpness across cos=0.0–0.8 only because the method is invariant to cos(theta) by construction — it never engages the superposition axis it claims to cover.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Genuinely hand-built with no learned parameters, but uses oracle features + pinv rather than demonstrating understanding of the superposition geometry.

visual_judgement.note: Four legible panels, but the headline 'Superposition robustness' table cell reads from payload (absent) instead of metrics, so it renders 0.000 — contradicting the central claim.

visualisation_rationale.note: README justifies the four panels and ties them to baseline/metrics, but doesn't acknowledge the viz omits the residual-stream geometry that the goal hinges on.

notes: The attempt achieves perfect benchmark numbers but does so by reading ground-truth feat_A/feat_B and constructing key vectors directly through W_Q/W_K pseudo-inverses, explicitly bypassing the e_A/e_B superposition directions that are the entire point of the goal. The headline robustness=1.0 is therefore an artifact of the method being invariant to cos(theta), not evidence of a NOT surviving superposition. Compounded by a visualization bug that renders the robustness cell as 0.000, this is a contrived solution that does not address the question.
